mitre_techniques.description: Attempt to gain access via brute-force password guessing

mitre_techniques.name: /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow
mitre_techniques.description: Access password files on Linux

mitre_techniques.description: Use non-standard protocols for C2

mitre_techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
mitre_techniques.description: Use application protocols for C2 communication

mitre_techniques.name: Encrypted Channel
mitre_techniques.description: Encrypt C2 communications

explanation: ⚠️ THREAT INTEL MATCH: Process 'hydra' communicated with known malicious IP 45.33.32.156, which is listed in threat intelligence feeds as a critical-confidence indicator of compromise. This likely represents active C2 (Command and Control) communication. Immediate action: terminate the connection, isolate the host, dump process memory for analysis, and check other hosts for connections to the same IP.

mitre_techniques.name: Application Layer Protocol
mitre_techniques.description: Use application protocols for C2 communication

mitre_techniques.name: Protocol Tunneling
mitre_techniques.description: Tunnel C2 through legitimate protocols

mitre_techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
mitre_techniques.description: Exfiltrate data over the C2 channel

explanation: Process wget communicated with external IP 1.1.1.1 using application-layer protocols (T1071). This may indicate C2 communication or data exfiltration. Action: capture network traffic for analysis, block the destination IP, and review DNS logs for domain generation algorithm (DGA) patterns.

mitre_techniques.name: Unix Shell
mitre_techniques.description: Abuse of Unix shell for command execution

explanation: Unix shell execution detected via bash (T1059.004: Command and Scripting Interpreter: Unix Shell). While shell usage can be legitimate, the threat score of 52.99 indicates suspicious context. Action: review the command history, check for downloaded scripts, and verify the user's identity.

Action: immediately terminate the process, block the IP at the firewall, and conduct a full host forensic analysis.

lateral_detections.description: Suspicious tool masscan connecting to new host 23.129.64.100

explanation: Network scanning tool masscan was detected (T1046: Network Service Discovery). The attacker is mapping your network to identify vulnerable services on port 31337. Action: block the source IP 23.129.64.100, check firewall logs for scan patterns, and verify all exposed services are patched.
